2605.12265.title: How Useful Is Cross-Domain Generalization for Training LLM Monitors?
2605.12265.paper_summary: Using prompted language models as classifiers enables classification in domains with limited training data, but misses some of the robustness and performance benefits that fine-tuning can bring. We study whether training on multiple classification tasks, each with its own prompt, improves performance on new domains with new classification prompts. We show that such training partially generalizes to adjacent domains, improving classification performance on tasks that are unseen during training. However, we identify specific edge cases where the fine-tuned models fail to follow prompts, such as when the classification prompt changes completely while the data domain remains the same as during training. We show that classification training can be mixed with general instruction following training, and that (when done well) such training keeps the benefits of classification training and mitigates its generalization failures. Surprisingly, we see that this no-thinking supervised classification training can generalize to with-thinking classification and summarization, suggesting that no-thinking classification training might be instrumentally useful in building other kinds of classifiers and monitoring systems.
2605.12265.paper_summary_zh: 使用提示語言模型作為分類器可以在訓練數據有限的領域中進行分類，但錯過了微調所能帶來的一些穩健性和性能優勢。我們研究在多個分類任務上訓練，每個任務都有其自己的提示，是否能提高在新領域中使用新分類提示的性能。我們顯示這種訓練在相鄰領域中部分泛化，改善了在訓練期間未見過的任務的分類性能。然而，我們識別出一些特定的邊緣情況，微調模型未能遵循提示，例如當分類提示完全改變而數據領域與訓練期間相同時。我們表明，分類訓練可以與一般指令跟隨訓練混合，並且（如果做得好）這種訓練保持了分類訓練的優勢並減輕了其泛化失敗。令人驚訝的是，我們看到這種無思考的監督分類訓練可以泛化到有思考的分類和摘要，這表明無思考的分類訓練可能在構建其他類型的分類器和監控系統中具有實質性價值。
2605.12265.author: Sam Martin et.al.
